content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


September 10, 1990


Eileen E. Padberg
Eileen E. Padberg Consulting
2081 Business Center Drive, Suite 125
Irvine, CA 92715

Re: Your Request For Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-521

Dear Ms. Padberg:

You have requested assistance under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because you have not
provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you
are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for
informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

You have asked whether a candidate who purchases a position
on a slate card produced by a slate mailer organization must
itemize the expenditures made by the slate mailer organization in
connection with the slate card.

CONCLUSION

Because slate mailer organizations have obligations under the
Act to register and file campaign disclosure statements itemizing,
among other things, expenditures made for the production of slate
mailers, a candidate or committee which purchases a position on a
slate card from a slate mailer organization is not required to
itemize expenditures made by the slate mailer organization. It is
sufficient for the candidate or committee, when reporting the

___________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Eileen E. Padberg
Page 2

payment to the slate mail organization on Schedule E of its
campaign disclosure statement, to indicate in the "Description"
section of Schedule E that the payee is a slate mailer
organization which files disclosure statements under the Political
Reform Act.

The conclusion in this letter replaces advice previously
provided in the Berman Advice Letter, No. A-88-283.

ANALYSIS

The advice provided in this letter applies only to state
mailers sent by state mailer organizations, as defined in the Act.
This advice does not apply to state mailers sent by a candidate or
committee, or any entity other than a slate mailer organization.

A "slate mailer" is "a mass mailing which supports or opposes
a total of four or more candidates or ballot measures." (Section
82048.3.)

The Act defines "slate mailer organization" as:

(a) "Slate mailer organization" means, except as
provided in subdivision (b), any person who, directly or
indirectly, does all of the following:

(1) Is involved in the production of one or more
slate mailers and exercises control over the selection
of the candidates and measures to be supported or
opposed in the slate mailers.

(2) Receives or is promised payments totaling five
hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year for
the production of one or more slate mailers.

(b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), a slate mailer
organization shall not include any of the following:

(1) A candidate or officeholder or a candidate's
or officeholder's controlled committee.

(2) An official committee of any political party.

(3) A legislative caucus committee.

(4) A committee primarily formed to support or
oppose a candidate, officeholder, or ballot measure.

(c) The production and distribution of slate mailers by
a slate mailer organization shall not be considered
making contributions or expenditures for purposes of
subdivision (b) or (c) of Section 82013. If a slate
mailer organization makes contributions or expenditures
other than by producing or distributing slate mailers,

Eileen E. Padberg
Page 3

and it reports those contributions and expenditures
pursuant to Sections 84218 and 84219, no additional
campaign reports shall be required of the slate mailer
organization pursuant to Section 84200 or 84200.5.

Slate mailer organizations have specific requirements under
the Act to file a Statement of Organization (Section 84108), and
to file periodic disclosure statements (Sections 84218, 84219, and
84220). On the disclosure statements, a slate mailer organization
must itemize, among other things, the total amount of
disbursements, and detailed information concerning each
disbursement of one hundred dollars ($100) or more.

With regard to the disclosure obligations of candidates and
committees, the Act requires that their campaign disclosure
statements provide, among other things, detailed information
concerning expenditures of $100 or more (Section 84211). In
addition, in the case of expenditures made on behalf of a
candidate or committee by an "agent or independent contractor,"
candidates and committees are required to disclose such
expenditures in the same detail as is required for expenditures
made directly by the candidate or committee (Section 84303;
Regulation 18431).

Notwithstanding the above, we believe the method of
disclosure described in the conclusion above is acceptable in this
situation because no additional information would be provided to
the public by requiring the candidate or committees to duplicate
the information disclosed by the slate mailer organization. The
public's interest in disclosure is fully served by the disclosure
required under the Act for slate mailer organizations.

If you have any questions about this letter, please call me
at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

It is sufficient for the candidate or committee, when reporting the

The conclusion in this letter replaces advice previously provided in the Berman Advice Letter, No. A-88-283.
sections.analysis:
The advice provided in this letter applies only to slate mailers sent by slate mailer organizations, as defined in the Act. This advice does not apply to slate mailers sent by a candidate or committee, or any entity other than a slate mailer organization.

A "slate mailer" is "a mass mailing which supports or opposes a total of four or more candidates or ballot measures." (Section 82048.3.)

The Act defines "slate mailer organization" as:

(a) "Slate mailer organization" means, except as provided in subdivision (b), any person who, directly or indirectly, does all of the following:

(1) Is involved in the production of one or more slate mailers and exercises control over the selection of the candidates and measures to be supported or opposed in the slate mailers.

(2) Receives or is promised payments totaling five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year for the production of one or more slate mailers.

(b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), a slate mailer organization shall not include any of the following:

(1) A candidate or officeholder or a candidate's or officeholder's controlled committee.

(2) An official committee of any political party.

(3) A legislative caucus committee.

(4) A committee primarily formed to support or oppose a candidate, officeholder, or ballot measure.

(c) The production and distribution of slate mailers by a slate mailer organization shall not be considered making contributions or expenditures for purposes of subdivision (b) or (c) of Section 82013. If a slate mailer organization makes contributions or expenditures other than by producing or distributing slate mailers,

and it reports those contributions and expenditures pursuant to Sections 84218 and 84219, no additional campaign reports shall be required of the slate mailer organization pursuant to Section 84200 or 84200.5.

Slate mailer organizations have specific requirements under the Act to file a Statement of Organization (Section 84108), and to file periodic disclosure statements (Sections 84218, 84219, and 84220). On the disclosure statements, a slate mailer organization must itemize, among other things, the total amount of disbursements, and detailed information concerning each disbursement of one hundred dollars ($100) or more.

With regard to the disclosure obligations of candidates and committees, the Act requires that their campaign disclosure statements provide, among other things, detailed information concerning expenditures of $100 or more (Section 84211). In addition, in the case of expenditures made on behalf of a candidate or committee by an "agent or independent contractor," candidates and committees are required to disclose such expenditures in the same detail as is required for expenditures made directly by the candidate or committee (Section 84303; Regulation 18431).

Notwithstanding the above, we believe the method of disclosure described in the conclusion above is acceptable in this situation because no additional information would be provided to the public by requiring the candidate or committee to duplicate the information disclosed by the slate mailer organization. The public's interest in disclosure is fully served by the disclosure required under the Act for slate mailer organizations.
